mission.title: Install and Configure Linode Kubernetes Engine (LKE)
mission.description: This mission guides you through installing the Linode Kubernetes Engine (LKE), a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies cluster management on Linode's infrastructure. It is ideal for users looking for a hassle-free Kubernetes experience with built-in cloud integration. Key trade-offs include reliance on Linode's infrastructure and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Linode CLI
steps.description:
The Linode CLI is required to interact with the Linode API for creating and managing your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -O https://linode.com/cli/linode-cli-1.20.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf linode-cli-1.20.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv linode-cli /usr/local/bin/

linode-cli configure

```

> Download the Linode CLI
> Extract the downloaded tarball
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH
> Configure the CLI with your Linode API token

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Linode with the specified version and node count.

```bash

linode-cli k8s cluster-create --label my-k8s-cluster --k8s-version 1.30 --node-count 3 --region us-east

```

> Create a cluster with 3 nodes in the US East region

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

linode-cli k8s kubeconfig-view my-k8s-cluster --text > ~/.kube/config

```

> Save the kubeconfig to your local machine

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step includes setting up monitoring and autoscaling for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/linode/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/monitoring/master/monitoring/monitoring.yaml

```

> Deploy the Cluster Autoscaler
> Deploy monitoring tools

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources, which may result in data loss.

```bash

linode-cli k8s cluster-delete my-k8s-cluster

```

> Delete the cluster and all associated resources

uninstall.title: Remove the Linode CLI
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Linode CLI from your system.

```bash

sudo rm /usr/local/bin/linode-cli

```

> Remove the Linode CLI binary

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your data and verify the current cluster state.

```bash

kubectl get nodes -o wide

linode-cli k8s cluster-update my-k8s-cluster --k8s-version 1.30

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check the current state of the nodes
> Upgrade the cluster to the specified version
> Verify that the nodes are running the new version

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or if the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Verify the kubeconfig file and ensure the cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule new pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up the cluster by adding more nodes using 'linode-cli k8s cluster-update my-k8s-cluster --node-count <new-count>'.

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in 'Pending' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if there are insufficient resources or misconfigured node selectors.

**Fix:**
Check node availability and pod specifications for correct node selectors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the API token used for the Linode CLI is invalid or expired.

**Fix:**
Regenerate your Linode API token and reconfigure the Linode CLI.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Linode NodeBalancers, improved integration with Linode API.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Linode CLI installed and configured with a valid API token.
